page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 80 vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 320.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 75092.52 and a PassMark CPU score of 52649.44. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, registering 4129.09 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 213.45 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES cryptography. While multi-threaded operations like software compilation and database operations perform in the top tier, single-core metrics such as stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression remain in the average tier.

The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which may introduce latency tradeoffs depending on the storage tier selected. Additionally, while the server handles smaller language model inference adequately, text generation for large 70B models is weak at 2.55 tokens/sec due to the absence of hardware accelerators. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density, multi-threaded enterprise workloads that require high memory bandwidth and concurrent processing without GPU acceleration. It is appropriate for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 is an x86_64 general-purpose server configured with 80 dedicated vCPUs and 320.0 GB of RAM. Powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor, it features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, achieving a stress-ng multi-core score of 75092.52 and high memory read bandwidth of 213.45 GB/sec for large blocks. However, single-core performance and large LLM text generation represent clear performance tradeoffs. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for resource-dense, concurrent workloads. It is best suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 is an x86_64 server with 80 dedicated vCPUs and 320 GB RAM. It delivers strong multi-core and database performance but lacks bundled local storage and GPUs.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 features 80 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 320 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core x86_64 platform featuring balanced memory allocation and strong multi-threaded performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated vCPUs and 320 GB RAM on Intel Xeon architecture.
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Average single-core performance and weak large-model LLM generation.
Best suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation.
